findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "sec_report_search" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Use when you need narrative content from SEC filings — risk factors, MD&A, guidance language, deal terms, accounting policies, share structure. For consolidated…

name: run_sql
description:
PostgreSQL SELECT over financial / market / alt-data tables — returns structured rows.

Hard rules (query fails otherwise):
- SELECT only, no CTE (`WITH ... AS`) — use subqueries.
- Period columns are TEXT, not dates — `period_end` is 'YYYY-MM'. Compare as strings (`period_end >= '2024-01'`); a `::d

name: get_table_schema
description:
Use BEFORE run_sql when you're unsure which columns a table has.

Look up column definitions (name, type, description) for a data table.

name: fiscal_utility
description:
Use to convert between fiscal year/quarter and calendar months for a given ticker.
Companies have different fiscal year starts (Apple Sep, Nvidia Jan) — call this
before filtering on period_end columns.

Fiscal year ↔ calendar month bidirectional conversion. Forward: ticker + fiscal_year + fiscal_qu

name: list_tables
description:
List alternative-data tables under the given categories. Returns each table's name,
one-line purpose, and column names (call get_table_schema if you need column
types/comments). Batch up to 5 categories in one call.

Use this BEFORE run_sql when you want to explore alt-data — run_sql alone won't
tel

name: news_search
description:
Use for any news, event, development, or statement question about a company,
theme, or the market. For SEC filing narrative use sec_report_search instead.

Coverage is UNEVEN by market — the main thing to get right. About half of
US-listed companies have a news trail here; Japanese ones ~5%, because

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "sec_report_search" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Use when you need narrative content from SEC filings — risk factors, MD&A, guidance language, deal terms, accounting policies, share structure. For consolidated…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "data" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_tables · Private data: sec_report_search · Public sink: sec_report_search, run_sql, fiscal_utility · Destructive: sec_report_search, run_sql.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_tables · Private data: sec_report_search · Public sink: sec_report_search, run_sql, fiscal_utility · Destructive: sec_report_search, run_sql

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
